title: Static Methods in a Java Interface
description: A visual representation showing how static methods are defined and called within a Java interface, highlighting their key characteristics and differences from default and abstract methods.
elements: Static Method Definition (e.g., 'static int add(int a, int b)')
relationships: Static method is defined and implemented directly inside the interface
Calling code invokes the static method using the interface name (e.g., MathOperations.add(5, 3)) without needing an implementing class
